prompt: Add .github/workflows/update-the-valley.yml to cosmo, mirroring the existing update-klaus.yml pattern exactly (read it first). Trigger: repository_dispatch types [the-valley-updated] plus workflow_dispatch. Steps identical to update-klaus.yml (same pinned action shas, same app-token minting with APP_ID/APP_PRIVATE_KEY, same environment: automation): update-flake-lock scoped to 'inputs: the-valley', pr-title 'chore: update the-valley', labels dependencies+automated, then gh pr merge --auto --squash. One header comment: the dispatch arrives from the-valley's GitHub MIRROR (its canonical origin is the sovereign valley host; the mirror push triggers the notify workflow there), and the converge loop deploys the merged bump — end-to-end automatic from 'valley review [i]' on classic-laddie to deployed. Verify: zizmor passes on the new workflow (CI runs it), nix flake check unaffected. Normal PR flow (cosmo is GitHub-canonical). PR body: two sentences + note the owner must add the COSMO_DISPATCH_TOKEN secret on gunk-dev/the-valley before the loop is live.
